parameterGroups.label: Modulation
parameterGroups.rationale: Rate and Width control the internal LFO that modulates the delay time

parameters.name: Rate
parameters.description: LFO rate controlling the modulation speed

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: Modulation depth controlling the intensity of the pitch variation

parameterGroups.label: Delay
parameterGroups.rationale: Feedback and Delay control the delay line characteristics

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.description: Amount of signal fed back into the delay line for more pronounced sweeps

parameters.name: Delay
parameters.description: Base delay time that gets modulated by the LFO

gaps.question: What is the processing chain in the chorus? Is it a single modulated delay line per channel, or multiple voices? How is the wet signal mixed with the dry?
gaps.context: Need to understand the chorus topology for pseudo-code.

gaps.question: What kind of LFO drives the delay modulation? Sine, triangle? Is there a phase offset between left and right channels for stereo widening?
gaps.context: The LFO shape affects the character of the chorus effect.

gaps.question: The Delay parameter has range 0-1. What does this map to in actual delay time (milliseconds)? What is the maximum delay buffer size?
gaps.context: Users need to understand the actual time range.

gaps.question: Where is the feedback applied in the signal chain? Is it fed back before or after mixing with dry?
gaps.context: Determines whether high feedback creates flanging effects.

gaps.question: The Rate parameter has range 0-1. What does this map to in Hz?
gaps.context: Users need to know the actual modulation speed range.

gaps.question: Is the delay line interpolation linear or cubic? Per-sample processing?

notes: Chorus has no modulation chains exposed to the user - the modulation is handled by an internal LFO. All parameters use NormalizedPercentage mode (0-1). Known scriptnode equivalent: fx.chorus.
